# localecheck

> Locale-correctness API and MCP server for AI agents. Gives agents verified,
> authoritative answers to the "boring" international data they otherwise
> hallucinate: dates, phone numbers, currency formatting, VAT/sales-tax by date,
> public holidays, and address parsing. Coverage: UK + USA (v1), expanding.

Agents are systematically wrong outside US-English defaults: they read 03/04 as
March 4 in the UK, apply today's VAT rate to a historical invoice, format €1.234,50
as $1,234.50, and miss bank holidays when computing delivery SLAs. localecheck
replaces guesses with checked answers and flags impossible inputs instead of
fabricating them.

## Tools / endpoints

- parse_date(input, locale): ambiguous date -> ISO 8601; INVALID for impossible dates. Respects en-GB day-first vs en-US month-first.
- validate_phone(number, region): -> E.164 + line type, via libphonenumber. INVALID if not valid.
- format_currency(amount, currency, locale): locale-correct currency string, via CLDR.
- tax_rate(country, date, state): UK standard VAT for a given date (handles historical/temporary changes); US national 0, state base sales tax by state code.
- is_holiday(date, country, subdiv): national public-holiday check. GB defaults to England; subdiv selects SCT/WLS/NIR or US states.
- next_holiday(country, after, subdiv): next public holiday on/after a date.
- parse_address(input): UK/US address -> {country, postcode, city} with a confidence flag.

## Install

- MCP server (live on npm): `npx -y localecheck` — add to Claude Desktop, Cursor, or any MCP client.
- Library: `npm install localecheck` then `import { vatRate } from "localecheck"`.
- Python package: in the repo (packages/localecheck-python); PyPI release coming soon.
- Source: https://github.com/qinisolabs/localecheck

## Docs

- README: https://github.com/qinisolabs/localecheck/blob/main/README.md
- Benchmark (how often LLMs get this wrong): https://github.com/qinisolabs/localecheck/blob/main/benchmark.html
